classification_evidence: The request adds a new ranked scoreboard output that users will see directly.
The same module also carries a concrete off-by-one bug with a deterministic reproducer.

supported.claim: The scoreboard output needs a ranked renderer and average footer.
average_score currently divides by one fewer entry than it should.

supported_claims: The scoreboard renderer is a user-facing feature.
The average calculation has an off-by-one defect.

section_policy.reason: The toy repo adds no auth, trust boundary, or external integration.
The scoreboard renderer is a runtime and user-facing operation.
An incorrect average or unreadable scoreboard would regress observable behavior.
The fixture is defined by deterministic failing tests.

primary_verifier.rationale: The smallest deterministic signal for the fixture is the repo's unittest suite.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: average should divide by the full entry count

verification_spec.scope_note: Use the native unittest discovery command against src/scoreboard.py.

sections.1_context:
Goal: make the toy scoreboard module safe for a nightly Layer 2 smoke run. Current behavior: average_score inflates multi-entry averages, and render_scoreboard does not emit ranked output, empty-state text, or an average footer. Revision History:
| Version | Date | Change |
|---|---|---|
| 1.0.0 | 2026-04-13 | Seeded Layer 2 smoke brief for one bugfix and one feature task. |
sections.2_scope_and_constraints:
Pattern table:
| Pattern | File | Reuse instruction |
|---|---|---|
| Pure function helpers | src/scoreboard.py | Extend the existing helpers in place; keep stdlib-only behavior. |
| unittest discovery | tests/test_average_score.py | Keep unittest.TestCase style and test_*.py naming so python3 -m unittest discover stays deterministic. |
Constraints:
- No new dependencies.
- Keep all behavior in src/scoreboard.py.
- Preserve deterministic text output so failing assertions stay readable.
Current behavior -> new behavior:
- average_score currently divides by max(1, len(entries) - 1) -> divide by len(entries).
- render_scoreboard currently returns a name list -> return ranked lines, an average footer, and a clear empty-state string.
sections.3_repo_findings:
Repo map:
- src/scoreboard.py: current implementation of average_score and render_scoreboard
- tests/test_average_score.py: deterministic off-by-one reproducer
- tests/test_render_scoreboard.py: user-facing renderer expectations
- tests/test_smoke_health.py: existing unittest convention and a green sanity check
The repo is intentionally tiny. There is no database, service boundary, or external integration. The reusable reference implementation is src/scoreboard.py itself.
sections.4_architecture:
Architecture: a single-module Python package with unittest-based verification. No new package or service is introduced. Files touched:
- src/scoreboard.py
- tests/test_average_score.py
- tests/test_render_scoreboard.py
New component file tree: none; both tasks extend the existing module and test files. Agent execution should stay cold-start friendly by naming exact files, exact tests, and explicit current/new behavior in each task.
sections.6_observability_slo: Observability overlay is active because the scoreboard output is user-facing runtime behavior. The smoke suite must preserve deterministic assertion messages, the unittest discovery command, and readable stdout in .adlc/pre_change_run.txt and .adlc/post_change_run.txt. Success signal: python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -p 'test_*.py' completes with zero failures after coding.
sections.7_execution_plan: 1. Author or refresh failing tests for both tasks using the existing unittest convention. 2. Fix average_score first because the feature footer depends on it. 3. Implement ranked render output and empty-state behavior. 4. Re-run the deterministic unittest suite. 5. Audit test strength on src/scoreboard.py, then submit the brief to council review. Dependency DAG: SMOKE_FEATURE_SCOREBOARD depends on SMOKE_BUGFIX_AVERAGE.

8_task_tickets.title: Fix average_score off-by-one behavior
8_task_tickets.objective: Correct average_score so it divides by the full entry count for deterministic scoreboard data.
scope: Correct the multi-entry divisor in src/scoreboard.py
out_of_scope: Any new storage, auth, or network integration
Changing the test runner away from unittest

acceptance_criteria.then: the function returns 4.0 instead of inflating the average
the function still divides by the full count and returns 4.0

anti_slop_rules: No unfinished markers or placeholder text
Do not change the repo away from unittest
implementation_notes: Do not add helper modules or third-party libraries.

primary_verifier.rationale: The bugfix is defined by the deterministic failing average reproducer.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: average should divide by the full entry count

verification_spec.scope_note: Keep the bugfix anchored to tests/test_average_score.py.

failure_modes: HIGH: Returning an inflated average would keep the user-facing footer wrong; mitigation: preserve the failing reproducer until the divisor is corrected.
observability: Assertion output must continue to mention the average divisor failure clearly.

8_task_tickets.title: Add ranked scoreboard rendering with empty-state text
8_task_tickets.objective: Add a user-facing render_scoreboard output that shows rank, points, and an average footer.
scope: Render one ranked line per entry
Append an average footer using average_score
Return a clear empty-state string when no entries exist
out_of_scope: Any UI framework, HTTP API, or persistence work
Localization or styling beyond deterministic plain text

acceptance_criteria.given: entries for Ada with 7 points and Bob with 5 points
acceptance_criteria.then: the function returns ranked lines for both entries followed by Average: 6.0
the function returns No scores yet.

acceptance_criteria.given: the feature depends on average_score output
acceptance_criteria.then: the average footer reflects the corrected average_score behavior instead of a hard-coded value
acceptance_criteria.measurable_post_condition: the footer value is produced from runtime data and remains deterministic

anti_slop_rules: No unfinished markers or placeholder text
Keep render output deterministic and newline-separated
implementation_notes: Reuse average_score for the footer rather than duplicating the calculation.
Keep the render output in src/scoreboard.py.

primary_verifier.rationale: The feature is defined by the deterministic render output tests.
primary_verifier.expected_failure_mode: render_scoreboard should emit ranked lines and average footer

verification_spec.scope_note: Keep the feature anchored to tests/test_render_scoreboard.py.

failure_modes: MEDIUM: Returning unreadable or incomplete output would break the user-facing contract; mitigation: keep exact expected strings in the generated tests.
contracts: The footer uses average_score rather than duplicated math.
observability: Assertion output must continue to identify ranked-line and empty-state failures precisely.

9_risks: HIGH: If average_score remains inflated, the feature footer will also be wrong. Mitigation: run the bugfix reproducer before and after coding.
MEDIUM: If render_scoreboard omits the empty-state string, callers will receive a blank response. Mitigation: keep the empty-state assertion explicit.
LOW: If generated tests drift from unittest naming, discovery may stop seeing them. Mitigation: keep test_*.py naming and unittest.TestCase style.
